prompt:
Goal: Create a 3:2 horizontal surreal Renaissance-collage poster about a digital 404 void, combining antique manuscript design, classical sculpture, cybernetic ruin, and obsolete computer UI.

Canvas: Square-ish 3:2 editorial poster composition on aged beige parchment paper with stains, fibers, faint grid lines, registration marks, thin drafting guides, tiny technical annotations, and a torn-paper lower edge revealing a strip of dark glitchy code/static underneath.

Main subject: A colossal reclining Renaissance marble statue of a bearded male figure, like a fallen classical god or philosopher, filling the lower two-thirds of the image diagonally from bottom left to upper right. The stone body is cracked, eroded, and broken open in multiple places, exposing dense gold-and-black circuitry, server boards, mechanical ribs, and electronic components inside. Thick black and gray cables coil around and through the statue, wrapping the torso, shoulder, arm, and legs like vines. Add heavy industrial metal braces and rails strapped across the figure, with small triangular markings and embedded panels. The statue’s head is tilted back toward the upper right, eyes closed or lifeless, with a weathered curly beard and hair.

Background and symbols: Place one large muted burnt-orange red sun disk behind the statue’s head and upper torso, slightly right of center. Scatter exactly 44 small black hourglass icons across the upper half of the poster, varying size and opacity, with a few larger hourglass symbols near the top right and upper center. Behind the figure, include a distant broken stone wall or ruined fortress silhouette running horizontally.

Typography and left panel: On the upper left, set a large refined serif headline in tall condensed Renaissance editorial type: “SCROLLING INTO THE VOID”, stacked in four lines, with “VOID” in muted rusty red and the other words in dark charcoal. Add tiny technical labels around it such as “FIG. 7”, “MEMENTO MORI”, “SYSTEM FAILURE”, and faint Latin-like filler text. Below the headline, create a thin vertical index with exactly 3 numbered entries labeled 01, 02, and 03, each with tiny unreadable pseudo-academic notes. Add delicate compass, measurement, and chart lines in the left and center background.

Error UI detail: Near the lower right over the cables and statue, place one small retro Windows-style dialog box with a blue title bar reading “System Error”. The dialog message should say: “The universe has encountered a fatal error. Reality cannot continue.” Include exactly one centered gray button labeled “OK”.

Visual style: Hyper-detailed surreal collage, neo-Renaissance engraving meets cyberpunk archaeology, photorealistic textures mixed with vintage print design, muted sepia palette with charcoal black, stone gray, oxidized gold circuitry, and a single rusty red accent. Use dramatic but soft museum-like lighting, crisp detail, layered paper textures, and high-end editorial poster composition.

Constraints: Keep the composition spacious and horizontal with strong negative space in the upper left; no modern glossy sci-fi neon except subtle glitch colors at the torn bottom edge; no extra people; no watermark; preserve the exact main headline text SCROLLING INTO THE VOID, the red sun color muted burnt-orange red, the statue subject reclining bearded Renaissance marble god, the error message The universe has encountered a fatal error. Reality cannot continue., and the poster material aged beige parchment paper.
